title: WASM exception thrown by _decode escapes before r.free() is called, leaking the WASM object
description: If `_decode(jpeg, denom)` throws (malformed JPEG, allocation failure, WASM trap), the `try` block is never entered so `r` is never assigned and `r.free()` in `finally` cannot run — but more importantly if `_decode` returns successfully yet `r.data`, `r.width`, or `r.height` property access throws (e.g. WASM returns null/undefined), the `finally` still runs fine. The real gap is that a throw inside `_decode` itself means no cleanup is possible because `r` is not in scope. Additionally, any error thrown by `_decode` propagates as an untyped WASM RuntimeError with no context about which JPEG caused it, giving callers no actionable information. Consider wrapping the `_decode` call in the try block and providing a structured error message.

title: No guard against _decode returning null/undefined before property access
description: The WASM binding `_decode` is typed as `any`. If the underlying Rust function returns `None` or panics in a way that wasm-bindgen maps to `undefined`, the call `r.data` on line 21 will throw `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined`, and `r.free()` in `finally` will also throw `TypeError`, masking the original error. A null/undefined check on `r` before destructuring would produce a clear error instead of a confusing secondary exception.

title: No size guard on jpeg input — arbitrarily large buffers passed directly to WASM
description: The `jpeg` parameter is passed to WASM without any length check. A caller passing a multi-hundred-MB buffer will cause WASM memory allocation to fail or the WASM heap to grow unboundedly. This is especially relevant in a Node environment where the buffer may come from untrusted sources. A simple max-size guard (e.g. 64 MB) would prevent runaway WASM memory growth and provide a clear error to callers.
code_excerpt:
export function decodeJpegScaled(jpeg: Uint8Array, denom: Denom = 1): JpegDecodeResult {
  const r = _decode(jpeg, denom);

title: No typed error class for JPEG decode failures — callers cannot distinguish decode errors from programming errors
description: All errors from `decodeJpegScaled` propagate as raw WASM RuntimeError or TypeError objects. Callers have no reliable way to distinguish a malformed-JPEG error (recoverable) from a programming error like passing wrong types. A `JpegDecodeError` class wrapping the WASM error with `cause` and a `code` field would let the scheduler or session layer handle decode failures selectively without catching all errors.
